    compat ¶

    Compatibility tools.

    This module contains adapters for making River estimators compatible with other libraries, and vice-versa whenever possible. The relevant adapters will only be usable if you have installed the necessary library. For instance, you have to install scikit-learn in order to use the compat.convert_sklearn_to_river function.

    Modules:

    • river_to_sklearn –
    • sklearn_to_river –

    Classes:

    • River2SKLClassifier –

      Compatibility layer from River to scikit-learn for classification.

    • River2SKLClusterer –

      Compatibility layer from River to scikit-learn for clustering.

    • River2SKLRegressor –

      Compatibility layer from River to scikit-learn for regression.

    • River2SKLTransformer –

      Compatibility layer from River to scikit-learn for transformation.

    • SKL2RiverClassifier –

      Compatibility layer from scikit-learn to River for classification.

    • SKL2RiverRegressor –

      Compatibility layer from scikit-learn to River for regression.

    Functions:

    • convert_river_to_sklearn –

      Wraps a river estimator to make it compatible with scikit-learn.

    • convert_sklearn_to_river –

      Wraps a scikit-learn estimator to make it compatible with river.

    River2SKLClassifier ¶

    River2SKLClassifier(river_estimator: Classifier)

    Bases: River2SKLBase, ClassifierMixin

    
              flowchart TD
              vendor.river.river.compat.River2SKLClassifier[River2SKLClassifier]
              vendor.river.river.compat.river_to_sklearn.River2SKLBase[River2SKLBase]

                              vendor.river.river.compat.river_to_sklearn.River2SKLBase --> vendor.river.river.compat.River2SKLClassifier
                


              click vendor.river.river.compat.River2SKLClassifier href "" "vendor.river.river.compat.River2SKLClassifier"
              click vendor.river.river.compat.river_to_sklearn.River2SKLBase href "" "vendor.river.river.compat.river_to_sklearn.River2SKLBase"

    Compatibility layer from River to scikit-learn for classification.

    Parameters¶

    river_estimator

    Methods:

    • fit –

      Fits to an entire dataset contained in memory.

    • partial_fit –

      Fits incrementally on a portion of a dataset.

    • predict –

      Predicts the target of an entire dataset contained in memory.

    • predict_proba –

      Predicts the target probability of an entire dataset contained in memory.

    fit ¶

    fit(X, y)

    Fits to an entire dataset contained in memory.

    Parameters¶

    X array-like of shape (n_samples, n_features). y array-like of shape n_samples.

    Returns¶

    self

    partial_fit ¶

    partial_fit(X, y, classes=None)

    Fits incrementally on a portion of a dataset.

    Parameters¶

    X array-like of shape (n_samples, n_features). y array-like of shape n_samples. classes Classes across all calls to partial_fit. This argument is required for the first call to partial_fit and can be omitted in the subsequent calls. Note that y doesn't need to contain all labels in classes.

    Returns¶

    self

    predict ¶

    predict(X)

    Predicts the target of an entire dataset contained in memory.

    Parameters¶

    X array-like of shape (n_samples, n_features).

    Returns¶

    Predicted target values for each row of X.

    predict_proba ¶

    predict_proba(X)

    Predicts the target probability of an entire dataset contained in memory.

    Parameters¶

    X array-like of shape (n_samples, n_features).

    Returns¶

    Predicted target values for each row of X.

    Compatibility layer from River to scikit-learn for transformation.

    Parameters¶

    river_estimator

    Methods:

    • fit –

      Fits to an entire dataset contained in memory.

    • partial_fit –

      Fits incrementally on a portion of a dataset.

    • transform –

      Predicts the target of an entire dataset contained in memory.

    fit ¶

    fit(X, y=None)

    Fits to an entire dataset contained in memory.

    Parameters¶

    X array-like of shape (n_samples, n_features). y array-like of shape n_samples.

    Returns¶

    self

    partial_fit ¶

    partial_fit(X, y=None)

    Fits incrementally on a portion of a dataset.

    Parameters¶

    X array-like of shape (n_samples, n_features). y array-like of shape n_samples.

    Returns¶

    self

    transform ¶

    transform(X)

    Predicts the target of an entire dataset contained in memory.

    Parameters¶

    X array-like of shape (n_samples, n_features)

    Returns¶

    Transformed output.

    SKL2RiverClassifier ¶

    SKL2RiverClassifier(
    estimator: ClassifierMixin, classes: list
)

    Bases: SKL2RiverBase, Classifier

    
              flowchart TD
              vendor.river.river.compat.SKL2RiverClassifier[SKL2RiverClassifier]
              vendor.river.river.compat.sklearn_to_river.SKL2RiverBase[SKL2RiverBase]

                              vendor.river.river.compat.sklearn_to_river.SKL2RiverBase --> vendor.river.river.compat.SKL2RiverClassifier
                


              click vendor.river.river.compat.SKL2RiverClassifier href "" "vendor.river.river.compat.SKL2RiverClassifier"
              click vendor.river.river.compat.sklearn_to_river.SKL2RiverBase href "" "vendor.river.river.compat.sklearn_to_river.SKL2RiverBase"

    Compatibility layer from scikit-learn to River for classification.

    Parameters¶

    estimator A scikit-learn regressor which has a partial_fit method. classes

    Examples¶

    from river import compat from river import evaluate from river import metrics from river import preprocessing from river import stream from sklearn import linear_model from sklearn import datasets

    dataset = stream.iter_sklearn_dataset( ... dataset=datasets.load_breast_cancer(), ... shuffle=True, ... seed=42 ... )

    model = preprocessing.StandardScaler() model |= compat.convert_sklearn_to_river( ... estimator=linear_model.SGDClassifier( ... loss='log_loss', ... eta0=0.01, ... learning_rate='constant' ... ), ... classes=[False, True] ... )

    metric = metrics.LogLoss()

    evaluate.progressive_val_score(dataset, model, metric) LogLoss: 0.198029

    SKL2RiverRegressor ¶

    SKL2RiverRegressor(estimator: BaseEstimator)

    Bases: SKL2RiverBase, Regressor

    
              flowchart TD
              vendor.river.river.compat.SKL2RiverRegressor[SKL2RiverRegressor]
              vendor.river.river.compat.sklearn_to_river.SKL2RiverBase[SKL2RiverBase]

                              vendor.river.river.compat.sklearn_to_river.SKL2RiverBase --> vendor.river.river.compat.SKL2RiverRegressor
                


              click vendor.river.river.compat.SKL2RiverRegressor href "" "vendor.river.river.compat.SKL2RiverRegressor"
              click vendor.river.river.compat.sklearn_to_river.SKL2RiverBase href "" "vendor.river.river.compat.sklearn_to_river.SKL2RiverBase"

    Compatibility layer from scikit-learn to River for regression.

    Parameters¶

    estimator A scikit-learn transformer which has a partial_fit method.

    Examples¶

    from river import compat from river import evaluate from river import metrics from river import preprocessing from river import stream from sklearn import linear_model from sklearn import datasets

    dataset = stream.iter_sklearn_dataset( ... dataset=datasets.load_diabetes(), ... shuffle=True, ... seed=42 ... )

    scaler = preprocessing.StandardScaler() sgd_reg = compat.convert_sklearn_to_river(linear_model.SGDRegressor()) model = scaler | sgd_reg

    metric = metrics.MAE()

    evaluate.progressive_val_score(dataset, model, metric) MAE: 84.501421

    convert_river_to_sklearn ¶

    convert_river_to_sklearn(estimator: Estimator)

    Wraps a river estimator to make it compatible with scikit-learn.

    Parameters¶

    estimator

    convert_sklearn_to_river ¶

    convert_sklearn_to_river(
    estimator: BaseEstimator, classes: list | None = None
)

    Wraps a scikit-learn estimator to make it compatible with river.

    Parameters¶

    estimator classes Class names necessary for classifiers.
